title: 6.1.2 Flow of Energy and Matter through Ecosystems
description: Energy flow, 10% law, ecological pyramids — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: What is the ultimate source of energy in life?
What process do producers use to trap solar energy?
Can energy be recycled in an ecosystem?
options: Only solar energy can be recycled
Only chemical energy can be recycled

questions.question: Which of the following can be recycled within an ecosystem?
How do nutrients in food behave in an ecosystem?
options: They are used once and lost forever
They recycle and are reused over and over again
They only flow in one direction
questions.correctAnswer: They recycle and are reused over and over again

questions.question: What two processes result in the recycling of nutrients in an ecosystem?
What do plants synthesize from simple inorganic matter?
Which organisms obtain energy by breaking down organic matter from dead producers and consumers?
What key nutrients do decomposers return to the soil?
As energy flows from one trophic level to the next, what happens to the amount of energy?
Approximately what percentage of energy in grass is turned into biomass in the body of a cow that feeds on it?
What happens to about 90% of the energy in grass that a cow eats?
options: It is lost as heat, used for activities, or removed as undigested food
It is passed to the next consumer
questions.correctAnswer: It is lost as heat, used for activities, or removed as undigested food

questions.question: According to the energy transfer diagram in Figure 6.4, what percentage of energy is assimilated as biomass in the cow?
According to the energy transfer diagram in Figure 6.4, what percentage of energy is lost as energy for heat and to do work?
What is an ecological pyramid?
options: A graphical representation of feeding relationships in the form of a pyramid
A physical structure built in ecosystems
questions.correctAnswer: A graphical representation of feeding relationships in the form of a pyramid

questions.question: What is used to construct an ecological pyramid?
What does each horizontal bar in an ecological pyramid represent?
options: Organisms of a specific trophic level
questions.correctAnswer: Organisms of a specific trophic level

questions.question: What is the order of bars in an ecological pyramid based on?
Which trophic level is at the bottom of an ecological pyramid?
Which trophic level is at the top of an ecological pyramid?
How many types of ecological pyramids are there?
What are the three types of ecological pyramids?
options: Pyramid of water, air, and soil
Pyramid of energy, number, and biomass
Pyramid of plants, animals, and minerals
Pyramid of producers, consumers, and decomposers
questions.correctAnswer: Pyramid of energy, number, and biomass

questions.question: The pyramid of energy is always which shape?
options: Upright and narrowing sharply from base to top
questions.correctAnswer: Upright and narrowing sharply from base to top

questions.question: Why is the pyramid of energy always upright?
options: Because energy increases at each level
Because there is commonly 90% energy loss at each trophic level
questions.correctAnswer: Because there is commonly 90% energy loss at each trophic level

questions.question: What percentage of solar energy are producers capable of converting by photosynthesis?
What is biomass?
options: The total energy of an organism
The weight of living organic matter contained or assimilated to the body of an organism per unit time
The amount of water in an organism
questions.correctAnswer: The weight of living organic matter contained or assimilated to the body of an organism per unit time

questions.question: What does the length of the bar in an ecological pyramid show?
options: The amount of energy, biomass, or number of organisms at each trophic level
questions.correctAnswer: The amount of energy, biomass, or number of organisms at each trophic level

questions.question: Which type of ecological pyramid can be inverted?
What determines whether a pyramid of biomass or number is upright or inverted?
options: The type of ecosystem and the particular food chain
questions.correctAnswer: The type of ecosystem and the particular food chain

questions.question: In Figure 6.6 showing an aquatic food chain pyramid of number, which organisms are at the base?
In Figure 6.6 showing an aquatic food chain pyramid of number, which organisms are in the middle?
In Figure 6.6 showing an aquatic food chain pyramid of number, which organisms are at the top?
In the idealized pyramid of energy in Figure 6.5, how much sunlight energy is shown at the base?
Why is the number of trophic levels usually limited to 3 or 4?
options: Because energy decreases at each level and eventually insufficient energy remains
Because there is no more space in the ecosystem
questions.correctAnswer: Because energy decreases at each level and eventually insufficient energy remains

questions.question: What does the pyramid of energy primarily represent?
Ecological pyramids can also be constructed to represent which two other aspects at each trophic level?
The bars at the base of an ecological pyramid always represent which organisms?
What happens to energy that is not assimilated as biomass when a herbivore eats a producer?
options: It is lost as heat and used for activities
questions.correctAnswer: It is lost as heat and used for activities
